# Schema: Agent Handover Brief
<!-- Defines the structure of agent-brief.md generated by /pm-agent-brief. -->
<!-- Optimized for AI coding agents: Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot Workspace, Devin, etc. -->

Agent briefs are written to `features/[feature-name]/agent-brief.md`.

---

## Design Principles

A good agent brief is:

1. **Bounded** — the agent knows exactly where to start and stop
2. **Testable** — acceptance criteria are observable, not interpretive
3. **Contextual** — the agent has the product and technical context to make good decisions
4. **Explicit about what not to do** — prevents unintended changes
5. **Clear about when to stop and ask** — prevents the agent from guessing on ambiguous decisions

---

## Required Fields

| Section | Required | Notes |
|---------|----------|-------|
| Task summary | Yes | 1–2 sentences. Precise outcome, not implementation approach. |
| Product context | Yes | Product description, affected user segment |
| Technical context | Yes | Repo, relevant files, tech stack, related systems |
| In scope | Yes | Specific list of what to build |
| Out of scope | Yes | Explicit list of what NOT to touch |
| Requirements (must implement) | Yes | Specific and testable. Use checklist format. |
| Anti-requirements (must NOT do) | Yes | At least 2 items |
| Acceptance criteria | Yes | Each item observable and testable |
| Edge cases | Yes | Table format: Scenario → Expected behavior |
| Constraints | Yes | Style, performance, accessibility, security |
| Definition of done | Yes | Checklist including tests and analytics |
| Escalation triggers | Yes | Situations where agent should stop and ask |

---

## Quality Checklist

Before handing an agent brief to an AI agent, verify:

- [ ] Task summary has no ambiguous verbs (avoid "handle", "manage", "support" — use "display", "validate", "write", "return")
- [ ] Every acceptance criterion can be verified without PM judgment (no "looks good" criteria)
- [ ] Out-of-scope list includes at least the 2 most tempting things to accidentally change
- [ ] Escalation triggers cover the top 2 decision points the agent would otherwise guess at
- [ ] Tech context points to specific file paths, not just "the frontend"
- [ ] Definition of done includes specific analytics event names, not just "instrument metrics"

---

## Token Efficiency Tips

For agents with limited context windows:
- Keep task summary to 2 sentences max
- Use bulleted lists over paragraphs
- Reference file paths directly instead of describing structure
- Put acceptance criteria before technical context (agent should know the goal before the constraints)

---

## Example Brief (condensed)

```markdown
# Add keyword search to Order History — Agent Brief

## Task Summary
Add a keyword search field to the Orders page that filters orders by order number,
item name, or seller name. Results update on keystroke (debounced 300ms).

## In scope
- Search input component on `/orders` page
- Backend query endpoint: GET /api/orders?q=[keyword]
- Empty state when no results

## Out of scope
- Do NOT modify the order detail page
- Do NOT change the existing order list sorting
- Do NOT add pagination changes

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Search input renders on the Orders page above the order list
- [ ] Typing in the search field filters the list within 300ms (debounced)
- [ ] Filtering works on: order_number, item_name, seller_name fields
- [ ] Empty state component renders when results array is empty
- [ ] Clearing the search input restores the full order list
- [ ] All existing orders page tests pass

## Escalation Triggers
Stop and ask the PM if:
- Search should persist across page refreshes (not specified)
- Mobile layout needs different treatment than desktop
```
